# Test the bash command in mcpscripttest

# Test simple echo
bash 'echo "Hello from bash"'
stdout 'Hello from bash'

# Test using environment variables
env TEST_VAR=World
bash 'echo "Hello $TEST_VAR"'
stdout 'Hello World'

# Test command substitution
bash 'echo "Current dir: $(pwd)"'
stdout 'Current dir:'

# Test piping commands
bash 'echo "one\ntwo\nthree" | grep two'
stdout 'two'

# Test with stdin
setstdin 'input data'
bash 'cat'
stdout 'input data'

# Test error conditions
! bash 'exit 1'

# Test multiple commands
bash 'echo first; echo second'
stdout 'first'
stdout 'second'

# Test with file operations
bash 'echo "test content" > test.txt'
bash 'cat test.txt'
stdout 'test content'
bash 'rm test.txt'

# Test command that uses MCP tools (commented out - needs mcp-spy condition)
# [has_mcp_spy] bash 'echo "{}" | mcp-spy --help'
# [has_mcp_spy] stdout 'Usage:'
